Activity: Andrea Dietrich liked this
...
Excited to welcome the Quotient AI team to Databricks! 

Their platform monitors agent behavior in production, catches issues like hallucinations and broken reasoning before users do, and uses those signals to continuously improve performance over time. 

Databricks already provides powerful tools for evaluating and improving AI agents. With Quotient, we're taking that even further across Genie, Genie Code, and Agent Bricks.

results.title: Hershey Company AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
The Hershey Company has undergone a comprehensive AI and digital transformation, positioning itself as a technology leader in the consumer goods industry. Under the leadership of its first Chief Technology Officer Deepak Bhatia (hired from Amazon in 2023) and Chief Information Officer Gene Kholodenko, Hershey has implemented AI across multiple business functions[1]. The company leverages AI for supply chain optimization, predictive maintenance, marketing personalization, and product innovation, with initiatives like Atlas - an AI-driven innovation tool that accelerates product development from five months to five weeks[2]. Hershey's digital backbone, built on SAP S/4 HANA and SAP Datasphere, enables AI-powered analytics and automation at scale[3]. The company has invested over $250 million in its Advancing Agility and Automation Initiative through 2026, focusing on digitization, automation, and AI integration to optimize procurement, manufacturing, and planning processes[4].
...

results.title: Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics (Hershey, PA, US) | Hirevector
highlights:
Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics (Hershey, PA, US) | Hirevector
...
# Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics (Hershey, PA, US)
...
, 2026
...
- Location : Hershey, PA
- Department : Consumer Connections
- Summary of Role :
...
- The Analyst, Commerce Insights & Analytics supports the measurement, reporting, and insight generation for Hershey’s omnichannel commerce and retail media initiatives. This role plays a critical part in connecting the dots between retail media investments, shopper behavior, and sales performance across eCommerce and brick-and-mortar retail environments. The ideal candidate is data-curious, detail-oriented, and eager to support analytics that improve campaign performance, inform strategic decisions, and uncover opportunities to drive conversion at the digital and physical shelf. This position works closely with Commerce, Sales, Media, and Shopper Marketing teams to ensure analytics are integrated into planning and post-campaign evaluation.
...
- Performance Reporting & Dashboard: Build and maintain recurring reports and dashboards to track media effectiveness, ROAS, conversion, and shopper engagement across key retail partners. Support updates to internal performance tools using Excel, Power BI, or other visualization platforms
...
- Retail Media & Sales Data Integration: Assist in collecting and synthesizing data from RMNs (Amazon, Walmart Connect, Kroger, Instacart, etc.), sales portals, and syndicated sources (Circana, NielsenIQ). Help identify correlations between media activity and retail performance at the brand, campaign, or retailer level
...
- Campaign Measurement Support: Contribute to campaign wrap-ups by compiling performance metrics, retailer feedback, and shopper response. Support test-and-learn programs by helping track variables and analyze performance against control groups
...
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with Commerce Media, Sales, and Shopper Marketing teams to align on reporting requirements and campaign objectives. Partner with internal teams and agency partners to ensure consistent data inputs and definitions
...
- Insights Generation & Communication: Translate data into clear summaries and highlight key insights and implications for internal stakeholders. Help identify opportunities to improve investment efficiency and optimize campaign performance
...
- Desired knowledge, skills, and abilities :
...
- Strong Excel and analytical skills; familiarity with visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau is a plus
- Understanding of retail media platforms and eCommerce metrics
- Detail-oriented with a passion for accuracy and insight generation
- Strong communication skills, especially in distilling data into clear, actionable takeaways
- Ability to manage multiple workstreams and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
- Experience working with retail data (Amazon Brand Analytics, Walmart Luminate, Circana, etc.) preferred
...
- Minimum Education and Experience Requirements :
...
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Analytics, Economics, or a related field
...
- At least 1-3 years of experience in marketing analytics, eCommerce reporting, or shopper insights
- CPG, agency, or retail experience is a plus

highlights:
# Digital Shelf Consultant (Hershey, PA, US)
...
- The Digital Category Consultant is responsible for shaping and scaling best‑in‑class digital shelf strategies that drive profitable growth across Hershey’s total snacking portfolio and priority customers. Serving as the Category Leadership authority on the digital shelf, this role partners closely with internal and external cross‑functional teams to develop, test, learn, and scale online merchandising principles across top e‑commerce platforms including Amazon, Walmart, Target, Kroger, and OnDemand retailers.
...
- This role leads digital test‑and‑learn agendas and translates data, shopper insights, and experimentation into actionable, retailer‑specific strategies. The Digital Category Consultant owns the evolution of “perfect screen” practices across total snacking — including PDPs, taxonomy, online assortment, search, and content — ensuring Hershey’s categories win at the moment of purchase.
...
- The ideal candidate is deeply data‑curious, forward‑thinking, and experimentation‑driven, with a passion for turning insights into scalable solutions. ## The Hershey Company
...
The Hershey Company is an industry-leading snacks company headquartered in Hershey, Pennsylvania, recognized for bringing goodness to the world through its iconic brands and extraordinary commitment to helping children succeed. Hershey is well-known for its wide range of chocolate and confectionery products, driven by a culture that values inclusion, employee development, and sustainable practices to ensure a positive impact on the community and the planet.

...
• Lead Dot’s brand equity strategy, including positioning, brand purpose, messaging architecture, visual identity, tone of voice, and brand guardrails that support the Pretzels & Bold Snacking growth agenda. • Translate Dot’s long-term growth ambition into annual and multi-year activation plans that drive household penetration, awareness, trial, repeat, and brand power. • Steward Dot’s creative work, including campaign development, brand world/visual identity system work, packaging communication, and asset toolkits. • Partner with media, shopper, sales, category management, insights, innovation, commercialization, finance, supply chain, and external agency partners to build integrated plans across core business, innovation, tentpole moments, and key customer programs. • Develop demand-creation strategies by translating consumer tensions, cultural moments and category barriers into clear marketing actions. • Guide brand-building activation across key growth platforms and occasions, including everyday snacking and key cultural moments. • Monitor brand health, business performance, competitive activity, retail execution, media effectiveness, and cultural relevance to identify risks, opportunities, and optimization recommendations for senior leadership. • Lead development of executive-ready recommendations, briefs, plans, and presentations that clearly connect consumer insight, brand strategy, activation choices, financial impact, and executional needs.

# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.

# Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist
...
The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.
...
- As a Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects. You will be expected to follow processes and practices that allow your models to be incorporated into our machine learning platform for production execution and monitoring, however, initial exploratory data analysis allows for more flexible experimentation to discover solutions to the business problems presented.

You will play a key role in supporting the attribute extraction platform and catalog data quality solutions — adding capabilities and driving adoption of GenAI-powered solutions to increase the coverage and accuracy of the Walmart's product catalog. The Data Science and ML Engineering community at Walmart Global Tech is active in most of the Hack events, utilizing the petabytes of data at our disposal, to build some of the coolest ideas. highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
